channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Distinct Patterns of Mobility Recovery After Stroke Using Routine Clinical Data
item.description: A retrospective cohort study of 750 adults with stroke identified distinct mobility recovery trajectories during the first 180 days post-stroke using Growth Mixture Modeling based on Activity Measure for Post-Acute Care (AM-PAC) Basic Mobility scores.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Aspirin Matches Rivaroxaban for Blood Clot Prevention After Joint Replacement, Suggests Study
item.description: The EPCAT III randomized trial found an aspirin-only regimen was as effective and safe as rivaroxaban for preventing symptomatic venous thromboembolism after total hip or knee arthroplasty, with no clinically significant difference in bleeding risk.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Beyond "Biomedical-Plus": Reclaiming the biopsychosocial model in musculoskeletal physiotherapy.
item.description: This perspective piece argues that while the biopsychosocial (BPS) model is endorsed in musculoskeletal physiotherapy, its implementation often devolves into "biomedical-plus" care. It proposes three foundational conditions—professional Confidence, role Clarity, and Compassionate communication—for physiotherapists to achieve a more authentic BPS application.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study of 137 patients referred to advanced orthopedic virtual physical therapy found that 70.9% completed full care episodes, exceeding national benchmarks (71% vs. 43%). Among completers, 96% achieved Minimal Clinically Important Differences.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of Combined Biopsychosocial Interventions for Chronic Low Back Pain: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis will evaluate the effectiveness of combined biopsychosocial interventions compared to usual care, waiting-list controls, and active unimodal interventions in adults with chronic low back pain, aiming to reduce pain and functional disability.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Comparative efficacy of different joint mobilization techniques in shoulder adhesive capsulitis: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis will compare the clinical effects of different non-thrust joint mobilization approaches on objective and subjective outcomes in individuals with shoulder adhesive capsulitis, based on English-language RCTs published since 2000.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Early Weight-Bearing Speeds Recovery After Unstable Ankle Fracture Surgery: study
item.description: Early Weight-Bearing Speeds Recovery After Unstable Ankle Fracture Surgery: study ... # Early Weight-Bearing Speeds Recovery After Unstable Ankle Fracture Surgery: study ... .whatsapp.com/send?text=Early%2 ... Weight-Bearing ... Speeds%20Recovery%2 ... After%20Unstable%20Ankle%20Fracture ... 20Surgery ... 20study%20https%3A%2F%2Fmedicaldialogues.in%2Forthopaedics%2Fearly-weight-bearing-speeds-recovery-after-unstable-ankle-fracture-surgery-study-174806%20
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: The RESOLVE Trial: A Stepped-Wedge, Cluster-Randomized Clinical Trial Evaluating the Effects of Clinical Practice Guidelines and Psychologically Informed Physical Therapy on Low Back Pain Outcomes in Service Members and Veterans.
item.description: A stepped-wedge, cluster-randomized trial evaluating a clinical practice guideline and psychologically informed physical therapy (CPG+PIPT) implementation strategy found it reduced LBP-related disability, pain, and healthcare utilization in military and veteran settings.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Long-term benefits of ultrasound-guided percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation for chronic lateral elbow pain: a randomized controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized, double-blinded, sham-controlled trial evaluating ultrasound-guided percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (PENS) combined with exercise for chronic lateral elbow pain found significant long-term benefits in pain intensity, pain-related disability, and psychological outcomes compared to sham PENS.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Moderate, Home-Based Exercise Proven Safe for Aortic Dissection Survivors, find researchers
item.description: Researchers have found that moderate, home-based exercise is safe for survivors of aortic dissection, providing important insights for their rehabilitation and long-term management.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: [Correspondence] From targets to minutes: making movement advice actionable
item.description: This correspondence suggests shifting physical activity messaging from aspirational thresholds to achievable marginal gains, showing that small increases in activity among the least active can yield the greatest population benefit, aligning with WHO guidance.
item.author: The Lancet

item.title: Transcranial Direct Current Stimulation (tDCS) Application in Neurorehabilitation: A Beginner’s Guide for Clinicians
item.description: This beginner's guide for clinicians explains Transcranial Direct Current Stimulation (tDCS), a noninvasive brain stimulation technique that modulates brain excitability via subthreshold direct current, highlighting its accessibility and cost-effectiveness for neurorehabilitation.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: The association between diabetes mellitus and low back pain: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis confirmed a significant association between diabetes mellitus and low back pain, highlighting the comorbid prevalence and longitudinal relationship between these two conditions in adult populations.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Adherence to exercise intervention including pain threshold, exercise and physiotherapy sessions for patients with chronic rotator cuff tendinopathy.
item.description: An investigation into adherence to pain threshold protocols during a 26-week exercise intervention for chronic rotator cuff tendinopathy found adherence to exercise and physiotherapy sessions was high, but adherence to strict pain thresholds varied.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cost-effectiveness of Pilates compared with home-based exercises for people with chronic non-specific low back pain: a trial-based economic evaluation.
item.description: An economic evaluation alongside a randomized controlled trial in Brazil found Pilates to be cost-effective compared to home-based exercises for chronic non-specific low back pain, from both healthcare and societal perspectives, despite not being covered by the Brazilian healthcare system.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Acute Modulation of Lumbar Motor Unit Behavior During Experimentally Induced Low Back Pain: A Motor Unit Decomposition Analysis.
item.description: An analysis of experimentally induced acute low back pain in 33 healthy participants found that acute LBP altered lumbar motor unit discharge characteristics and reduced force steadiness during sustained spinal extension.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Exploration of lifestyle interventions for individuals with knee osteoarthritis: A scoping review.
item.description: A scoping review exploring lifestyle interventions for knee osteoarthritis identified a diverse range of interventions including exercise and weight management, highlighting outcome measures used for assessment across 36 studies (29 RCTs).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prevalence, Patterns, Treatment Outcomes and Costs of Physiotherapy-Managed Musculoskeletal Disorders in Nigeria.
item.description: A 10-year retrospective study of physiotherapy-managed musculoskeletal disorders (MSDs) in Nigeria assessed prevalence, patterns, treatment outcomes, and costs, finding a mean age of 47.8 years and predominantly female patients among 1582 cases.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Exploring facilitators and barriers to health behaviors in stroke survivors: a multi-perspective qualitative study of patients, families, and healthcare professionals.
item.description: A qualitative study involving stroke survivors, family caregivers, and healthcare professionals in China explored facilitators and barriers to post-stroke health behaviors related to secondary prevention, interpreting findings through the Adaptive Leadership Framework.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Transcutaneous spinal cord stimulation for stroke rehabilitation: a scoping review.
item.description: A scoping review mapping current evidence on transcutaneous spinal cord stimulation (tSCS) for stroke rehabilitation found fragmented evidence and methodological heterogeneity, focusing on study design, stimulation parameters, and reported effects.
item.author: PubMed
